metadata.title: Why one context flag flips every legal position to its mirror image
metadata.topic: Playbook polarity reversal in contract review
metadata.purpose: Playbook polarity — each substantive position is stored as a directed value anchored to a side flag. Review mode reads the flag before any clause, so the same text triggers opposite redlines.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro am_onyx) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.exclusions: redline drafting mechanics, how the flag is set, the cold-start interview
metadata.visual_object: A bilateral clause with two opposing annotation arrows (customer-favoring, vendor-favoring) that swap when the flag flips.
metadata.hook: The "correct" position on liability caps, indemnity, IP ownership, and termination reverses completely when one flag switches from customer to vendor.

beats.narration_text: A vendor MSA arrives. Customer-side: push the vendor to indemnify you, raise the cap, grant a broad IP license. Vendor-side on the identical clause: resist each one.
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: Liability direction should come from the contract language; this case shows it comes first from which side you occupy — why does flipping one flag change every downstream answer?
The "correct" position on liability caps, indemnity, IP ownership, and termination reverses completely when one flag switches from customer to vendor.
Playbook polarity — each substantive position is stored as a directed value anchored to a side flag. Review mode reads the flag before any clause, so the same text triggers opposite redlines.
beats.visual: A bilateral clause with two opposing annotation arrows (customer-favoring, vendor-favoring) that swap when the flag flips.
beats.scaffold_note: the resolving mechanism; orbits the visual object

beats.narration_text: §12 caps vendor liability at 1× fees. Customer-side: "too low — push to 2×." Vendor-side: "too high — resist." Same clause, opposite action.
Playbook polarity — each substantive position is stored as a directed value anchored to a side flag. Review mode reads the flag before any clause, so the same text triggers opposite redlines.
